tests.title: --bool flips a real bool prop (playOnLoad) and engine reads false
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints '...playOnLoad = false' + '✓ written (.bak saved)'; exit 0
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset('db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab'). 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab('db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab') -> rootUuid. 3) component_get_components(rootUuid) -> find the cc.SkeletalAnimation component. ASSERT its playOnLoad === false (was true on disk before the edit).
tests.cleanup: node_delete_node(rootUuid) (do NOT save scene); restore _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab from .bak; project_reimport_asset the prefab.
tests.notes: playOnLoad is a plain boolean SkeletalAnimation field, on the ROOT node component so it survives instantiation. Best full-native bool case.

tests.title: --bool true (and the '1' truthy form) on _enableMorph reads true engine-side
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints '..._enableMorph = false' + '✓ written'; exit 0
tests.nativeSteps: 1) reimport prefab. 2) instantiate -> rootUuid. 3) node_find_node_by_name('soldier') (or walk rootUuid children RootNode/soldier) -> uuid. 4) component_get_components(uuid) -> cc.SkinnedMeshRenderer. ASSERT _enableMorph === false (was true).
tests.cleanup: delete instantiated root node (no save); restore .bak; reimport.
tests.notes: Covers --bool 'false' literal -> JS false (resolveValueSpec: a[0]==='true'||a[0]==='1'). _enableMorph is a real engine bool on SkinnedMeshRenderer.

tests.title: --int sets an enum-backed int prop (_shadowCastingMode) and engine reads the int
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints '..._shadowCastingMode = 0'; exit 0
tests.nativeSteps: 1) reimport. 2) instantiate -> rootUuid. 3) resolve RootNode/soldier node uuid. 4) component_get_components -> cc.SkinnedMeshRenderer; ASSERT _shadowCastingMode === 0 (was 1 on disk). shadowCastingMode is an enum (ShadowCastingMode.OFF=0).
tests.notes: _shadowCastingMode is stored as a bare int (1) in the prefab — Math.trunc(Number) path. Engine exposes it as an enum value 0.

tests.title: --enum behaves identically to --int (truncates to int) on _shadowReceivingMode
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints '..._shadowReceivingMode = 0'; exit 0
tests.nativeSteps: 1) reimport. 2) instantiate. 3) resolve RootNode/soldier; component_get_components -> cc.SkinnedMeshRenderer; ASSERT _shadowReceivingMode === 0 (was 1).
tests.notes: --enum and --int share the same coercion (Math.trunc(n(a0))). Distinct flag, distinct prop so it doesn't collide with the --int test.

tests.title: --num writes a float into a Vec3 sub-field (_lpos.y) on root; engine reads the float
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints '#1._lpos.y = 2.5'; exit 0
tests.nativeSteps: 1) reimport. 2) instantiate -> rootUuid. 3) node_get_node_info(rootUuid); ASSERT position.y ≈ 2.5 (was 0.956 on disk). Use a float tolerance < 1e-4.
tests.notes: #1 is the root cc.Node (escape hatch, not a Comp.prop selector — set still requires kind==='property', which #1._lpos.y satisfies). --num keeps the fractional value (no trunc). _lpos.y is a real node transform engine reads as position.y.

tests.title: set --uuid <asset> writes {__uuid__} into _materials[0]; engine resolves the material
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints the {__uuid__:...} value; exit 0. NOTE the existing _materials[0] is ALREADY material/soldier.mtl (uuid 8a58ddec...), so the written bytes equal the old bytes -> expectChange is effectively a no-op-content rewrite; pick a DIFFERENT material if one exists, else treat as round-trip.
tests.nativeSteps: 1) reimport. 2) instantiate -> rootUuid. 3) resolve RootNode/soldier; component_get_components -> cc.SkinnedMeshRenderer; ASSERT _materials[0].__uuid__/sharedMaterials[0] resolves to material/soldier.mtl (uuid 8a58ddec-f437-40b9-8ec0-1fc87de97fb5).
tests.notes: set --uuid takes an ASSET (path/basename/uuid) and wraps {__uuid__}. To prove a CHANGE, first verify only one material exists; if a second material asset is available, swap to it and assert that one instead. Otherwise this validates the --uuid coercion + that the engine still loads the material (a malformed uuid would break instantiation).

tests.title: --uuid consumes exactly one token (arity 1) — a trailing flag is not swallowed
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; '[dry-run — not written]'; file bytes UNCHANGED (stat/byte-compare before vs after).
tests.notes: Confirms VALUE_ARITY uuid=1 and --dry-run path. No editor needed.

tests.title: --null nulls a __uuid__ ref prop (_defaultClip); engine instantiates with no default clip
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints '..._defaultClip = null'; exit 0
tests.nativeSteps: 1) reimport (a null clip ref must still load -> validity gate). 2) instantiate -> rootUuid. 3) component_get_components(rootUuid) -> cc.SkeletalAnimation; ASSERT defaultClip is null/empty (was a clip uuid e3553cad...@73b7f). 4) ASSERT instantiate succeeded (no load error).
tests.notes: --null sets JS null. _defaultClip is an optional asset ref so nulling is engine-safe. The reimport+instantiate doubles as the malformed-file gate.

tests.title: --str writes a string into the node _name (#1._name); engine reads the new name
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints '#1._name = "SoldierRenamed"'; exit 0
tests.nativeSteps: 1) reimport. 2) instantiate -> rootUuid. 3) node_get_node_info(rootUuid); ASSERT name === 'SoldierRenamed' (was 'soldier_t').
tests.notes: Exercises --str via the generic `set` op (distinct from the dedicated `rename` op which is a different group). #1._name is a property selector on the root node. Provides a clean native string readback.

tests.title: --str with an empty string writes '' (a[0] ?? '')
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; coir reports the set to '' (empty); dry-run, file unchanged. (If the shell drops the empty arg, pass --str '' explicitly.)
tests.notes: resolveValueSpec str returns a[0] ?? '' so a missing token yields '' (legal). _name on a component is a harmless string field. cli-exit only.

tests.title: --color #RRGGBB writes a wrapped cc.Color with a=255 (on-disk shape)
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; written. Then `edit get soldier_t:cc.SkeletalAnimation._sockets -o json` returns {__type__:'cc.Color', r:32, g:48, b:74, a:255}.
tests.cleanup: restore .bak (overwrites the clobbered _sockets); reimport. (Optional native gate: reimport must still succeed even though _sockets now holds a Color — coir doesn't type-check the target; if reimport errors, that's acceptable since we restore immediately.)
tests.notes: There is no native-visible builtin cc.Color prop on these two components that round-trips cleanly, so --color is verified by the on-disk SHAPE via `coir get` (the value-flag's job is to produce {__type__:cc.Color,r,g,b,a}). Target _sockets (an array we overwrite then restore) so we don't corrupt a real field permanently. p(0)=0x20=32, p(2)=0x30=48, p(4)=0x4a=74, a defaults 255.

tests.title: --color #RRGGBBAA parses the alpha channel
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; `edit get ... -o json` returns {__type__:'cc.Color', r:32, g:48, b:74, a:128}.
tests.notes: #hex with 8 digits -> a = p(6) = 0x80 = 128. The #hex single-token arity branch (max=1 once a[0] starts with #) is exercised here.

tests.title: --color r g b a (4-token form) writes the same cc.Color shape
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; `edit get ... -o json` returns {__type__:'cc.Color', r:10, g:20, b:30, a:40}.
tests.notes: Covers the non-# branch: arity 4, r/g/b from a[0..2], a from a[3]. Distinct from the #hex branch. Also implicitly: --color r g b (3 tokens) defaults a=255 — could add as a variant.

tests.title: --color r g b (3 tokens) defaults alpha to 255
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; `edit get ... -o json` returns {__type__:'cc.Color', r:1, g:2, b:3, a:255} (a[3] undefined -> 255).
tests.notes: Edge of the rgba branch: when a[3] is undefined alpha falls back to 255. Confirms VALUE_ARITY color=4 stops at end-of-args without erroring.

tests.title: --vec2 writes {__type__:cc.Vec2,x,y} (on-disk shape)
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; `edit get ... -o json` returns {__type__:'cc.Vec2', x:1.5, y:-2} (negatives ok via the arity-bounded grab).
tests.notes: No native-visible Vec2 builtin prop on these comps, so shape via `coir get`. Verifies arity 2 + negative-token handling (token doesn't start with -- so it's grabbed).

tests.title: --vec3 writes a cc.Vec3; engine reads it as the node scale (_lscale on root)
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints '#1._lscale = {Vec3 2,2,2}'; exit 0
tests.nativeSteps: 1) reimport. 2) instantiate -> rootUuid. 3) node_get_node_info(rootUuid); ASSERT scale === (2,2,2) (was 1,1,1).
tests.notes: Targets the node transform _lscale (a genuine cc.Vec3) so --vec3 gets a FULL native readback (set-scale op is a separate group; this proves the raw --vec3 flag via `set`). The {__type__:cc.Vec3} wrapper is what Cocos expects for a Vec3 field.

tests.title: --vec4 writes {__type__:cc.Vec4,x,y,z,w} (on-disk shape)
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; `edit get ... -o json` returns {__type__:'cc.Vec4', x:1, y:2, z:3, w:4}.
tests.notes: Arity 4. No native builtin Vec4 prop -> shape via `coir get`.

tests.title: --size writes {__type__:cc.Size,width,height}
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; `edit get ... -o json` returns {__type__:'cc.Size', width:100, height:200}.
tests.notes: Arity 2; note the keys are width/height (not x/y). Shape via `coir get`.

tests.title: --quat writes {__type__:cc.Quat,x,y,z,w}; engine reads it as the root rotation
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints '#1._lrot = {Quat ...}'; exit 0
tests.nativeSteps: 1) reimport. 2) instantiate -> rootUuid. 3) node_get_node_info(rootUuid); ASSERT rotation quat ≈ (0,0,0.7071068,0.7071068) within 1e-4 (a 90° Z). NOTE: set --quat does NOT also write _euler (unlike set-rot), so the engine's eulerAngles may be derived from the quat — assert on the quaternion, not euler.
tests.notes: Raw --quat flag verified on the real _lrot transform field for a full native readback. Distinct from set-rot (which is a different op-group and also writes _euler).

tests.title: --json sets a whole object value (a builtin-typed cc.Vec3 via JSON)
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints the object; exit 0. The builtin __type__ 'cc.Vec3' passes through unchanged (typeToken returns it).
tests.nativeSteps: 1) reimport. 2) instantiate -> rootUuid. 3) node_get_node_info(rootUuid); ASSERT position === (3,4,5) (was 0,0.956,1.402).
tests.notes: Exercises --json whole-object set where the inner __type__ is a BUILTIN (cc.Vec3) and must pass through resolveRawTypes untouched. Native readback via the position. Pass the JSON as ONE shell-quoted token.

tests.title: --json sets an array value (replace _clips with [] ) — engine loads with no clips
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; `edit get soldier_t:cc.SkeletalAnimation._clips -o json` returns []. Then a reimport must still succeed (validity gate).
tests.notes: --json '[]' covers the array branch of resolveValueSpec json. Verify the empty array on disk via `coir get` + a reimport gate (an empty _clips is engine-valid). Native component readback of _clips arrays is less reliable than the on-disk check.

tests.title: --json with a bare scalar (true) sets a boolean
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; `edit get ... -o json` returns false.
tests.nativeSteps: 1) reimport. 2) instantiate -> rootUuid. 3) component_get_components(rootUuid) -> cc.SkeletalAnimation; ASSERT playOnLoad === false.
tests.notes: JSON.parse('false') -> false (a scalar). Confirms --json handles scalars, not just objects/arrays. Same prop as set-bool-happy but via the --json path.

tests.title: get -o json output round-trips back into set --json unchanged
tests.expectedObservation: Step 1: `edit get soldier_t:cc.SkeletalAnimation._defaultClip -o json` -> the {__uuid__:...} JSON. Step 2: feed that exact JSON to `set --json`. Exit 0; a follow-up `get -o json` returns the IDENTICAL value. (Content is the same as before -> effectively a no-op rewrite.)
tests.cleanup: restore .bak (in case bytes were rewritten); reimport.
tests.notes: Proves the get(-o json) -> set --json round-trip contract for a __uuid__ ref value. A __uuid__ object has no __type__ so resolveRawTypes leaves it alone. Verifies value-shape stability.

tests.title: --json with invalid JSON exits 1, no write
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ bad value: invalid JSON: ...'; exit 1; file bytes UNCHANGED (byte-compare before/after).
tests.notes: GROUNDED: confirmed exit 1 with 'invalid JSON: Unexpected token' against the live fixture. resolveValueSpec exits BEFORE runEdit, so nothing is written.

tests.title: --json with an unknown custom __type__ class exits 1, no write
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ unknown __type__ class(es): NoSuchClass — no matching script asset in the project'; exit 1; file UNCHANGED.
tests.notes: GROUNDED: confirmed exit 1 with that exact message against the live fixture. resolveRawTypes collects the unknown class and editCli exits before runEdit.

tests.title: --color with a bad hex string exits 1, no write
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ bad value: bad hex color "#GGGGGG" (#RRGGBB or #RRGGBBAA)'; exit 1; file UNCHANGED.
tests.notes: GROUNDED: confirmed exit 1 with that exact message. The regex rejects non-hex; resolveValueSpec exits before any mutation.

tests.title: --color with a wrong-length hex (#12345) exits 1
tests.expectedObservation: stderr 'bad hex color'; exit 1; file UNCHANGED.
tests.notes: The regex requires exactly 6 or 6+2 hex digits; 5 digits fails. Edge variant of the bad-hex refusal.

tests.title: set with no value flag exits 1
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ set needs a value flag (--str/--int/...)'; exit 1; file UNCHANGED.
tests.notes: GROUNDED: confirmed exit 1 with the value-flag-list message. cmdSet bails when !flags.value before resolving the selector.

tests.title: --num with a non-numeric token exits 1 (clean error, not a crash)
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ bad value: "abc" is not a number'; exit 1; file UNCHANGED; no TypeError/stack trace.
tests.notes: resolveValueSpec's n() rejects NaN with a clean message and exits 1. Same path covers --int/--enum/--vec*/--color numeric args.

tests.title: set --uuid with no asset token exits 1, no crash
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ bad value: --uuid needs an asset'; exit 1; no 'TypeError/Cannot read'; file UNCHANGED.
tests.notes: resolveValueSpec uuid branch guards a missing a[0]. Mirrors the known fix#6 test.

tests.title: set --uuid <nonexistent asset> exits 2 (resolution failure)
tests.expectedObservation: stderr a resolveAsset not-found/candidates message; exit 2; file UNCHANGED.
tests.notes: The --uuid value flag resolves the asset via resolveAsset, which prints candidates and exits 2 on a miss (this happens during resolveValueSpec, before runEdit). Distinguishes a value-resolution exit (2) from a value-format exit (1).

tests.title: set on a node selector (not a property) exits 2
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ "soldier_t/RootNode/soldier" must select a property (…:Type.prop) for set'; exit 2; file UNCHANGED.
tests.notes: GROUNDED: confirmed exit 2 with that exact needProp message. runEdit refuses when resolveSelector.kind !== 'property'.

tests.title: set on a bare component selector (no .prop) exits 2 (needProp)
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '... must select a property (…:Type.prop) for set'; exit 2; file UNCHANGED.
tests.notes: A component selector resolves to kind==='component', not 'property' -> needProp exit 2. Distinct from the node-selector case.

tests.title: set with an unknown component type exits 2
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ node "soldier_t" has no component matching "cc.Nope._x"' + a candidate line 'cc.SkeletalAnimation'; exit 2; file UNCHANGED.
tests.notes: GROUNDED: confirmed exit 2 with the no-component-matching message and the cc.SkeletalAnimation candidate. resolveSelector returns {error,candidates}.

tests.title: set on a nested prop path that stops before a leaf exits 2 (setDeep error)
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ path "playOnLoad.nope" stops at "playOnLoad"' (setDeep error); exit 2; file UNCHANGED.
tests.notes: playOnLoad is a boolean, so descending into .nope hits setDeep's 'cur[k] not an object' guard -> error -> runEdit returns code 2. Covers the setDeep failure path for `set`.

tests.title: set accepts ANY value flag (no per-prop type policing) — int into a bool prop succeeds shape-wise
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; dry-run reports playOnLoad = 5 (set does NOT validate the flag against the field type — only node-ops like set-pos do). File UNCHANGED (dry-run).
tests.notes: Documents a real behavior contrast: unlike set-pos/set-active (which enforce WANT[op] flag types), the generic `set` writes whatever the flag produces. Important audit fact — verified as a dry-run so nothing is corrupted.

tests.title: --dry-run reports the change but leaves the file byte-identical
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; stdout contains '[dry-run — not written]'; sha/byte-compare of the file before vs after is IDENTICAL.
tests.notes: Snapshot the file bytes before, run, compare after. applyResult skips commitWrites when flags.dryRun.

tests.title: --backup writes <file>.bak before mutating
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; stdout '✓ written (.bak saved)'; _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab.bak EXISTS and equals the pre-edit content; `edit get ...playOnLoad -o json` returns false.
tests.cleanup: restore from the .bak (move .bak back over the file); reimport. (This test's own .bak is the restore source.)
tests.notes: Verifies the backup mechanism that ALL native tests rely on for cleanup. Run this early. Confirms get reads back the written value too.

tests.title: -o json emits a structured set result with dryRun flag
tests.expectedObservation: stdout is JSON: {file:'_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab', op:'set', selector:'soldier_t:cc.SkeletalAnimation.playOnLoad', prop:'playOnLoad', value:false, dryRun:true}. exit 0; file UNCHANGED (dry-run).
tests.notes: Confirms the -o json shape of `set` (applyResult json branch). Parse stdout and assert the keys. dry-run so no write/native needed.

tests.title: set is refused (exit 2) when the file changed on disk since read; --force overrides
tests.expectedObservation: When the on-disk mtime differs from read time, writeAtomic throws and commitOrExit prints '✗ file changed on disk since it was read...' and exits 2. With --force appended, exit 0 and the write applies.
tests.cleanup: if --force variant wrote, restore from a pre-made .bak; reimport.
tests.notes: Hard to trigger deterministically in a single coir invocation (read+write happen in one process). Practical native trigger: have the Cocos editor SAVE the prefab (touch mtime) between a coir read and write — or `touch` the file mid-flight. Lower priority; the unit suite doesn't cover --force for set. If infeasible to time, mark as documented/known rather than executed.

tests.title: rename a clean child node's _name; engine reports the new name
tests.expectedObservation: stdout '…sword _name = ...' style success + '✓ written (.bak saved)'; exit 0; the node previously named 'sword' is now '_name':'Blade'
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset('db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab') — must succeed (validity gate). 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab(prefabPath='db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab') → capture rootUuid. 3) node_find_node_by_name('Blade') → must return a node (engine built the renamed node); node_find_node_by_name('sword') under this instance → must NOT find it. ASSERT a 'Blade' node exists in the instantiated subtree and no 'sword'.
tests.cleanup: node_delete_node(rootUuid) (do NOT save scene); restore _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab from .bak (mv .bak over the file) + project_reimport_asset.
tests.notes: Rename a deep leaf so it can't be confused with the instantiate root. Grounded: path resolves (tree shows 'sword' #69).

tests.title: empty string '' is a legal _name (rename succeeds, writes empty name)
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0 (NOT the usage error — '' is accepted because nodeOp checks pos[3]===undefined, not falsy); the 'shield' node's _name becomes ''
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset(url) — must succeed (proves the empty-name file is valid). 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab → rootUuid. 3) node_find_node_by_name('shield') in the instance must NOT find it (it was renamed to ''); read the instantiated subtree (node_get_node_info(rootUuid) → walk RootNode children) and ASSERT one direct child of RootNode now has name '' where 'shield' used to be. cleanup after.
tests.notes: Quote the empty arg as '' on the shell. Confirms the editCli special-case (pos[3]===undefined guard) holds end-to-end in the real engine.

tests.title: ambiguous node selector (same-name siblings, no [i]) is refused, no write
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ "nested_t/" matches 2 same-name nodes — add [i]'; exit 2; file bytes UNCHANGED. (Ambiguity resolution precedes the instance guard, so this errors on ambiguity, not on instanceGuard.)
tests.cleanup: none (no write); assert file bytes identical via a pre/post byte compare (e.g. shasum) — no .bak needed since nothing should be written.
tests.notes: Grounded: `get nested_t/` already returns exit 2 with this exact message. soldier_t has NO same-name siblings, so the only real ambiguous-node target in the fixtures is nested_t's two empty-named children.

tests.title: set-active --bool false on a child node; engine node is inactive
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; '_active' on the target node set to false (writes literal boolean false, NOT string)
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset(url). 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab → rootUuid. 3) node_find_node_by_name('shield01') within the instance (note: two 'shield01' exist at different paths #49 and #74 — pick the one that is a direct child of RootNode; verify via parent). 4) node_get_node_info(thatUuid) → ASSERT active === false. cleanup.
tests.notes: Grounded: target #74 'soldier_t/RootNode/shield01' resolves (exit 0). Use the RootNode child (#74), distinguishable from the deep #49 by parent name. --bool parses 'false' to literal false (a[0]==='true'||a[0]==='1').

tests.title: set-active --bool true restores an active node (true literal path)
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; '_active' stays true (the node was already active so the JSON value is unchanged, but the op still re-serializes and writes — verify engine sees active:true)
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset(url). 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab → rootUuid. 3) node_find_node_by_name('soldier') (RootNode child #3). 4) node_get_node_info → ASSERT active === true. cleanup.
tests.notes: expectChange=false in the sense the boolean value is identical to original; the file is still rewritten (re-serialized) so a byte-diff WILL differ — this test verifies the engine-side value, not byte-stability. Covers the --bool 'true' coercion branch.

tests.title: set-active rejects a non-bool value flag (--vec3) with exit 1, no write
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ set-active needs a bool value (a different --flag was given)'; exit 1; file bytes UNCHANGED
tests.cleanup: none (no write); byte-compare pre/post to confirm unchanged.
tests.notes: Grounded: exact message + exit 1 confirmed via --dry-run probe. WANT['set-active']=['bool']; the wrongFlag guard fires in editCli BEFORE runEdit, so no resolution/write occurs.

tests.title: set-active rejects --str true (still wrong flag, not bool) exit 1
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ set-active needs a bool value (a different --flag was given)'; exit 1; file UNCHANGED
tests.notes: Distinct from the --vec3 case: confirms even a string 'true' is rejected — the value flag's TYPE must be bool, not its content. Grounded via dry-run probe.

tests.title: set-active with no value flag → noValue error exit 1, no write
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ set-active needs a value flag (--str/--int/--num/--bool/...)'; exit 1; file UNCHANGED
tests.notes: Covers the !flags.value branch (EM.noValue) distinct from the wrongFlag branch.

tests.title: set-layer --int changes _layer; engine reports the new layer
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; '_layer' on RootNode becomes 33554432 (DEFAULT layer; original was 1073741824/UI_2D). Value written as a literal integer.
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset(url). 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab → rootUuid. 3) node_find_node_by_name('RootNode') → uuid. 4) node_get_node_info(uuid) → ASSERT layer === 33554432 (if node info exposes layer; else component_get_component_info / scene_get_scene_hierarchy fallback). cleanup.
tests.notes: Grounded: original RootNode _layer=1073741824, dry-run with --int 33554432 succeeds exit 0. 33554432 = cc.Layers.Enum.DEFAULT, a real valid layer so the engine accepts it cleanly. If node_get_node_info omits 'layer', this test falls back to cli-reread (coir get soldier_t/RootNode:?... actually _layer is a node field — use `edit get soldier_t/RootNode -o json` and assert _layer) — but PREFER native since the reimport+instantiate already validates.

tests.title: set-layer --dry-run locates but does not write (exit 0, file unchanged)
tests.expectedObservation: stdout '…RootNode _layer = 33554432   [dry-run — not written]'; exit 0; file bytes UNCHANGED (no .bak created either)
tests.cleanup: none; byte-compare pre/post to confirm unchanged.
tests.notes: Grounded: dry-run output + exit 0 confirmed. Verifies the dry-run no-write contract for a node op without invoking the editor.

tests.title: set-pos --vec3 with negatives writes _lpos; engine local position matches
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; '_lpos' becomes {__type__:'cc.Vec3',x:5,y:-3,z:-2.5}; engine local position of the 'soldier' node = (5,-3,-2.5)
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset(url). 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab (NO position arg, so the root's transform is from the file). 3) node_find_node_by_name('soldier') (RootNode child #3) → uuid. 4) node_get_node_info(uuid) → read its LOCAL position; ASSERT position ≈ (5,-3,-2.5) within 1e-4. cleanup.
tests.notes: Verify on a CHILD (not the root) so the value can't be confounded by any instantiate-position applied to the prefab root. Tests integer + negative + fractional negative. Grounded selector resolves.

tests.title: set-pos rejects --int (must be vec3) exit 1, no write
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ set-pos needs a vec3 value (a different --flag was given)' (matches /vec3/); exit 1; file UNCHANGED
tests.notes: Mirror of the unit test fix#2/#7 but grounded on the real fixture. Confirms a scalar can't be stuffed into a Vec3 field.

tests.title: set-scale --vec3 writes _lscale; engine local scale matches
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; '_lscale' becomes {__type__:'cc.Vec3',x:2,y:2,z:2}; engine local scale of 'soldier' = (2,2,2)
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset(url). 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab → rootUuid. 3) node_find_node_by_name('soldier') → uuid. 4) node_get_node_info(uuid) → read LOCAL scale; ASSERT ≈ (2,2,2). cleanup.
tests.notes: Child node so scale is its own local. Grounded: 'soldier' #3 has _lscale present.

tests.title: set-scale non-uniform with a negative axis (mirror) is written verbatim
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; '_lscale' = {x:1.5,y:-1,z:0.25}; engine local scale of 'sword' = (1.5,-1,0.25)
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset(url). 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab → rootUuid. 3) node_find_node_by_name('sword') → uuid. 4) node_get_node_info → ASSERT local scale ≈ (1.5,-1,0.25). cleanup.
tests.notes: Exercises a negative (mirroring) + fractional non-uniform scale to prove the engine round-trips arbitrary Vec3 components, not just uniform positives.

tests.title: set-rot single-axis Z=90 writes _euler AND the matching _lrot quat; engine confirms both
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; node._euler={x:0,y:0,z:90}; node._lrot≈cc.Quat(0,0,0.707107,0.707107); engine reports euler (0,0,90) AND quaternion (0,0,0.7071,0.7071)
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset(url). 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab → rootUuid. 3) node_find_node_by_name('soldier') → uuid. 4) node_get_node_info(uuid) → read rotation. ASSERT engine euler angles ≈ (0,0,90) AND, if quaternion exposed, rotation quat ≈ (0,0,0.7071068,0.7071068) within 1e-4. (Engine reconstructs euler from _lrot on load, so a CORRECT _lrot is what makes the engine report (0,0,90) — this is exactly why coir writes both; a stale/zero _lrot would show 0°.) cleanup.
tests.notes: Computed quat grounded via the actual eulerToQuat. The KEY native value here vs the unit test: the engine derives its displayed rotation from _lrot, so reading (0,0,90) back proves _lrot (not just _euler) was written correctly.

tests.title: set-rot single-axis X=90; engine quat ≈ (0.7071,0,0,0.7071)
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; node._euler={x:90,y:0,z:0}; node._lrot≈cc.Quat(0.707107,0,0,0.707107); engine euler ≈ (90,0,0)
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset(url). 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab → rootUuid. 3) node_find_node_by_name('sword') → uuid. 4) node_get_node_info → ASSERT engine euler ≈ (90,0,0) AND (if exposed) quat ≈ (0.7071068,0,0,0.7071068). cleanup.
tests.notes: Second single-axis (X) confirms the per-axis sign/order of eulerToQuat against the engine, not just Z. Grounded quat.

tests.title: set-rot multi-axis 30,60,90 → clean quat (0.5,0.5,0.5,0.5); engine confirms
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; node._euler={x:30,y:60,z:90}; node._lrot≈cc.Quat(0.5,0.5,0.5,0.5); engine quaternion ≈ (0.5,0.5,0.5,0.5)
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset(url). 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab → rootUuid. 3) node_find_node_by_name('shield') (RootNode child #64) → uuid. 4) node_get_node_info → read rotation quat; ASSERT ≈ (0.5,0.5,0.5,0.5) within 1e-4 (and/or euler ≈ (30,60,90)). cleanup.
tests.notes: Multi-axis is the strongest eulerToQuat ↔ engine Quat.fromEuler parity check: 30,60,90 produces the exact (0.5,0.5,0.5,0.5) quaternion (grounded by computing eulerToQuat). A wrong rotation-order in coir would NOT land on this clean value, so the engine readback is decisive. Note two 'shield' family names exist; #64 'shield' is a direct RootNode child, distinct from 'shield01'.

tests.title: set-rot requires --vec3 (euler); --quat is rejected exit 1, no write
tests.expectedObservation: stderr 'set-rot needs --vec3 <x> <y> <z> (euler degrees)'; exit 1; file UNCHANGED
tests.notes: Grounded: set-rot has its own guard (flags.value.type !== 'vec3') with a distinct message (no '✗' prefix). Confirms set-rot won't accept a raw quaternion — it only takes euler degrees.

tests.title: set-rot with no value flag → rotNeedsVec3 error exit 1, no write
tests.expectedObservation: stderr 'set-rot needs --vec3 <x> <y> <z> (euler degrees)'; exit 1; file UNCHANGED
tests.notes: set-rot's branch checks (!flags.value || type!=='vec3') so the missing-value case shares the rotNeedsVec3 message (not the generic noValue).

tests.title: node selector that doesn't exist → exit 2 with candidates, no write
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ no node "soldier_t/RootNode/Nope"' (selErr) plus indented candidate node paths; exit 2; file UNCHANGED
tests.notes: Covers the resolveSelector not-found path for a node op (the value flag is valid so we get past the flag guard into runEdit → selError, code 2). Distinguishes selector-resolution failure (exit 2) from value-flag failure (exit 1).

tests.title: a component selector given to a node op → needNode refusal exit 2
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ "soldier_t:cc.SkeletalAnimation" must select a node for this op'; exit 2; file UNCHANGED
tests.notes: Grounded: tree shows the root soldier_t carries cc.SkeletalAnimation. The selector resolves to a COMPONENT (kind!=='node'), so runEdit returns OM.needNode (code 2). Proves node ops reject component targets even when the component exists.

tests.title: rename a nested-prefab-instance node is refused (instanceGuard) exit 2
tests.expectedObservation: stderr '✗ "..." is (in) a nested prefab instance — edit its source prefab directly, not here'; exit 2; file UNCHANGED
tests.notes: nested_t node #2 (nested_t/[0]) is a [prefab instance] (grounded via tree -o json instance:true). Here [i] is supplied so resolution succeeds, then the editableGuard fires. Complements rename-ambiguous-refuse (which errors earlier, on ambiguity). A node-transforms op on an instance node must be blocked.

tests.title: scene-target parity: set-pos on a scene fixture node writes _lpos; engine confirms
tests.expectedObservation: exit 0; the targeted scene node's _lpos becomes (7,8,9); after scene_open_scene the engine node's local position = (7,8,9)
tests.nativeSteps: FIRST resolve the exact node selector: run `edit _coirtest/scene_t.scene tree` and pick a clean (non-instance) node — e.g. a 'Main Light'/'Main Camera' child of 'main' that is NOT one of the [prefab instance] nodes (main/[0],[1],[2] at #7/#44/#59). Then: 1) coir edit set-pos with --backup. 2) project_reimport_asset('db://assets/_coirtest/scene_t.scene'). 3) scene_open_scene('db://assets/_coirtest/scene_t.scene'). 4) node_find_node_by_name(<that node>) → uuid. 5) node_get_node_info → ASSERT local position ≈ (7,8,9). cleanup.
tests.cleanup: scene_close_scene WITHOUT scene_save_scene (do not persist editor changes); restore _coirtest/scene_t.scene from .bak + project_reimport_asset.
tests.notes: The single scene-target parity test for this group (rest target soldier_t per instructions). MUST first ground the exact non-instance node selector from `tree` before running — the placeholder 'main/Main Light' is illustrative; replace with a verified clean scene node. Verifies the same node-transform code path produces an engine-valid SCENE file, not just a prefab.

tests.title: Single-file swap-uuid rewrites all 3 material refs in soldier_t.prefab; engine re-reads + the soldier SkinnedMeshRenderer now points at the new material
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints '_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab: swap material/soldier.mtl → material/plane.mtl', '3 reference(s)', the 3 selector lines (soldier/shield/sword :cc.SkinnedMeshRenderer._materials.0), and '✓ written (.bak saved)'. After reimport+instantiate, the soldier/shield/sword nodes' SkinnedMeshRenderer _materials[0] resolves to plane.mtl (uuid 23e988d0-7168-4fe2-9d46-f29c114e9e33), NOT soldier.mtl.
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset('db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab') → must succeed (validity gate: a malformed swap would fail reimport). 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab('db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab') → newRootUuid. 3) scene_get_scene_hierarchy or node_find_node_by_name('soldier') under newRoot to get the soldier node uuid (path soldier_t/RootNode/soldier). 4) component_get_components(soldierNodeUuid) → find cc.SkinnedMeshRenderer; component_get_component_info → read _materials[0]. ASSERT its referenced material asset uuid == 23e988d0-7168-4fe2-9d46-f29c114e9e33 (plane.mtl), and != 8a58ddec-f437-40b9-8ec0-1fc87de97fb5 (soldier.mtl). 5) Optionally repeat for the shield + sword nodes (all 3 should now be plane.mtl). Corroborate engine-side with coir: `edit _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab get "soldier_t/RootNode/soldier:cc.SkinnedMeshRenderer._materials[0]"` prints 'material/plane.mtl'.
tests.cleanup: node_delete_node(newRootUuid) (do NOT save the scene). Restore the fixture: move _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab.bak → _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab (overwrite). project_reimport_asset('db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab') so the engine re-reads the restored bytes. Verify restored bytes byte-identical to pre-test (sha or `get` returns material/soldier.mtl again).
tests.notes: Primary swap happy-path. soldier.mtl appears exactly 3× in the fixture (verified). plane.mtl confirmed 0 refs in the fixture so the post-swap state is unambiguous. --backup gives the restore source.

tests.title: Single-file swap-uuid is a clean no-op (exit 0, no write) when the old asset is not referenced in the file
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints the head line then '(no references to "material/plane.mtl" in this file — nothing changed)'. No '✓ written'. File bytes UNCHANGED, no .bak created.
tests.cleanup: None needed (no write). Confirm no _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab.bak was created and the file mtime/bytes are unchanged.
tests.notes: plane.mtl confirmed 0 refs in fixture. count==0 ⇒ writes=[] ⇒ writeAtomic never called. No native verify needed (no engine-side change).

tests.title: swap-uuid onto the SAME asset (old===new) is a no-op even though the asset IS referenced (planSwapUuid short-circuits before counting)
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints head 'swap material/soldier.mtl → material/soldier.mtl' then '(no references ... nothing changed)'. count is forced to 0 by planSwapUuid's oldUuid===newUuid guard, so NO write and NO .bak even though soldier.mtl is referenced 3×.
tests.cleanup: None (no write). Verify file bytes unchanged and no .bak.
tests.notes: Distinct from norefs-noop: here the asset is present 3× but identity short-circuit prevents the rewrite. Matches fix#10 in cli.test.js.

tests.title: swap-uuid --dry-run reports the 3 references + each selector but writes nothing
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints '3 reference(s)   [dry-run — not written]' and the 3 selector lines (soldier_t/RootNode/soldier|shield|sword :cc.SkinnedMeshRenderer._materials.0). NO '✓ written'. File bytes UNCHANGED, no .bak.
tests.cleanup: None (dry-run never writes). Confirm bytes unchanged.
tests.notes: Confirms dry-run still does full resolution + location reporting (reuses edgeMaps to print sites) but skips commitWrites.

tests.title: swap-uuid --backup writes a .bak of the original before rewriting, then writes the new file
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints '✓ written (.bak saved)'. A file _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab.bak now exists and is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the pre-edit fixture (still contains soldier.mtl uuid 8a58ddec..., 3×). The live file now contains plane.mtl (23e988d0..., 3×) and zero soldier.mtl refs.
tests.cleanup: Restore: move _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab.bak → _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab (overwrite). project_reimport_asset('db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab'). Verify .bak removed and bytes restored.
tests.notes: Backup-mechanism check (separate from swap-single-happy's native readback). Assert .bak == original pre-edit bytes and live == swapped. cli-reread is enough; the swap correctness is already covered natively by swap-single-happy. If swap-single-happy already ran with --backup, this can be folded — keep as the explicit backup-byte-identity assertion.

tests.title: swap-uuid -o json --dry-run emits the structured result and writes nothing
tests.expectedObservation: stdout is one JSON line: {"file":"_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab","op":"swap-uuid","from":"material/soldier.mtl","to":"material/plane.mtl","fromUuid":"8a58ddec-f437-40b9-8ec0-1fc87de97fb5","toUuid":"23e988d0-7168-4fe2-9d46-f29c114e9e33","count":3,"dryRun":true}. File UNCHANGED.
tests.notes: Locks the JSON shape (file/op/from/to/fromUuid/toUuid/count/dryRun). Asserts count===3 and the exact uuids.

tests.notes: cmdSwapUuid guards !pos[2]||!pos[3] before any resolution → exit 1 (usage), distinct from the exit-2 resolution failures.

tests.title: swap-uuid refuses (exit 2) when the target file is not a prefab/scene (a .png)

tests.title: swap-uuid on a SCENE file (parity) rewrites a whole-asset prefab reference; the scene re-reads in the editor
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints '1 reference(s)' and the site 'main:cc.PrefabInfo.asset'... actually the scene ref is a node's _prefab/asset — coir reports the swapped count==1, then '✓ written (.bak saved)'. After reimport+open, the scene's reference that pointed at grassGoup.prefab (uuid 5e4d48c4-0e34-45af-a268-89485197e8bc) now points at islands.prefab (uuid ccc3a755-7d3d-4304-aa3b-ca4792d79d9f).
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset('db://assets/_coirtest/scene_t.scene') → must succeed (malformed scene would fail). 2) scene_open_scene('db://assets/_coirtest/scene_t.scene'). 3) scene_get_scene_hierarchy → confirm the scene loads without error and the referenced prefab node is present. Corroborate via coir: grep the scene raw for ccc3a755 (present) and 5e4d48c4 (absent), OR `edit _coirtest/scene_t.scene get` on the swapped site. Note: this is the single scene-target parity test; deep per-prop engine readback is the prefab tests' job. The strong gate here is: scene reimports + opens cleanly with the new ref.
tests.cleanup: scene_close_scene (do NOT save). Restore: move _coirtest/scene_t.scene.bak → _coirtest/scene_t.scene (overwrite). project_reimport_asset('db://assets/_coirtest/scene_t.scene'). Verify bytes restored.
tests.notes: Scene parity: same runEdit code path as prefab, only asset.type==='scene'. grassGoup.prefab confirmed 1 whole-asset ref in scene_t.scene; islands.prefab also already referenced once elsewhere in the scene — that's fine (swap only repoints the grassGoup uuid). Do NOT save the scene from the editor (would persist + change mtime).

tests.title: edit --all swap-uuid repoints material/soldier.mtl across EVERY prefab/scene (the fixture + the real soldier.prefab), 6 refs in 2 files; engine re-reads the fixture
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints 'swap material/soldier.mtl → material/plane.mtl   (project-wide)', '2 files, 6 references', two file lines '_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab 3 ✓' and 'model/helloWorld/folder-001/soldier.prefab 3 ✓', then '✓ 2 file(s) written (.bak saved)'. Both files now reference plane.mtl; .bak saved for each. After reimport+instantiate the fixture, soldier_t's SkinnedMeshRenderer materials are plane.mtl.
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset('db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab') → succeeds. 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab('db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab') → newRoot. 3) read soldier node's cc.SkinnedMeshRenderer _materials[0] (as in swap-single-happy) → ASSERT == plane.mtl uuid 23e988d0-7168-4fe2-9d46-f29c114e9e33. 4) ALSO project_reimport_asset('db://assets/model/helloWorld/folder-001/soldier.prefab') → must succeed (the real prefab was also rewritten — confirm it's still valid). Corroborate counts via coir: `edit --all swap-uuid material/soldier.mtl material/plane.mtl --dry-run` BEFORE the real run should report 2 files / 6 refs.
tests.cleanup: CRITICAL — restore BOTH files (the real asset was touched): move _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab.bak → _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab AND move model/helloWorld/folder-001/soldier.prefab.bak → model/helloWorld/folder-001/soldier.prefab (both overwrite). project_reimport_asset on BOTH urls. node_delete_node(newRoot). Verify both .bak removed and both files byte-restored (the real soldier.prefab must be identical to its pre-test bytes).
tests.notes: soldier.mtl is referenced ONLY by these 2 prefab files (verified via `uses`); there is no fixture-only asset, so --all unavoidably touches the real soldier.prefab. --backup + restore-both keeps it safe. Run the --dry-run hit-list first (swap-all-dryrun) to lock the exact file set before writing.

tests.title: edit --all swap-uuid --dry-run previews the project-wide hit list (2 files, 6 refs) and writes nothing
tests.expectedObservation: stdout JSON: op 'swap-uuid', scope 'all', from 'material/soldier.mtl', to 'material/plane.mtl', fromUuid 8a58ddec..., toUuid 23e988d0..., files [{file:'_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab',count:3},{file:'model/helloWorld/folder-001/soldier.prefab',count:3}], totalFiles 2, totalRefs 6, skipped [], dryRun true. NO files written, NO .bak anywhere.
tests.cleanup: None (dry-run). Verify both files unchanged and no .bak created.
tests.notes: Run this BEFORE swap-all-happy to confirm the exact blast radius. Asserts the project-wide enumeration (sorted by path) and that the real soldier.prefab is in scope. Also implicitly checks skipped===[] (no unparseable prefab/scene).

tests.title: edit --all rejects a non-swap op (set) with exit 1

tests.notes: cmdEditAll checks op!=='swap-uuid' first and exits 1 before any resolution/load. Selector ops can't generalize across files.

tests.title: edit --all rejects set-uuid (a selector op) with exit 1
tests.expectedObservation: stderr: '--all currently supports only swap-uuid (selector ops like "set-uuid" are per-file)'. exit 1. No file touched.
tests.notes: Confirms set-uuid specifically is rejected under --all (it is a per-file selector op). Complements swap-all-reject-set.

tests.title: edit --all swap-uuid is a no-op (exit 0, no writes) when nothing in the project references the old asset
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints '(no references to "material/plane.mtl" anywhere — nothing changed)'. exit 0. r.hits is empty so commitWrites is never called; no file written, no .bak anywhere.

If plane.mtl turns out referenced somewhere, substitute any material with 'used-by 0' from `analyze unused`/`uses`. Verify zero-hit precondition with the --dry-run first.

tests.title: set-uuid points soldier's SkinnedMeshRenderer _materials[0] at shield.mtl; engine re-reads + the material is the new one
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints '_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab: soldier_t/RootNode/soldier:cc.SkinnedMeshRenderer._materials[0] → model/helloWorld/folder-001/shield.mtl' + '✓ written (.bak saved)'. After reimport+instantiate, ONLY the soldier node's SkinnedMeshRenderer _materials[0] is shield.mtl (uuid 8e047178-f61c-4322-a2f6-d1adb28b6ae2); shield/sword nodes still reference soldier.mtl (8a58ddec...). The set is surgical — only that one property changed.
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset('db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab') → succeeds (malformed re-serialize would fail). 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab(...) → newRoot. 3) read soldier node cc.SkinnedMeshRenderer _materials[0] → ASSERT == shield.mtl uuid 8e047178-f61c-4322-a2f6-d1adb28b6ae2. 4) read the SISTER node soldier_t/RootNode/shield's SkinnedMeshRenderer _materials[0] → ASSERT still == soldier.mtl 8a58ddec... (proves set-uuid is surgical, not a global swap). Corroborate via coir `get`: soldier's _materials[0] prints model/helloWorld/folder-001/shield.mtl; shield's prints material/soldier.mtl.
tests.cleanup: node_delete_node(newRoot). Restore: move _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab.bak → _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab. project_reimport_asset(...). Verify bytes restored (soldier _materials[0] back to soldier.mtl).
tests.notes: set-uuid writes {__uuid__:ra.uuid} into the resolved property via setDeep + full re-serialize (parse-rewrite mode, not text patch). The sister-node assertion distinguishes set-uuid (one site) from swap-uuid (all sites). _materials[0] index confirmed in-range.

tests.title: set-uuid --dry-run resolves + reports the target but writes nothing
tests.expectedObservation: coir prints '... → model/helloWorld/folder-001/shield.mtl   [dry-run — not written]'. No '✓ written'. File UNCHANGED, no .bak. coir `get` on the same selector still returns material/soldier.mtl.

tests.title: set-uuid -o json --dry-run emits the structured result (selector/prop/to/toUuid)

tests.title: set-uuid refuses (exit 2) when the target asset does not resolve

tests.title: set-uuid refuses (exit 2) when the target file is not a prefab/scene (a .png)

tests.title: set-uuid with the asset arg missing prints usage and exits 1
tests.expectedObservation: stderr: 'set-uuid needs: <file> set-uuid <selector:Comp.prop> <asset>'. exit 1. No write.

tests.title: set ... --uuid (the generic set op with the --uuid value flag) writes {__uuid__} into a property — the alternative path to set-uuid

tests.title: set-uuid into an OUT-OF-RANGE array index (_materials[5]) succeeds at the CLI (exit 0, write) but produces a sparse/extended array — the engine validity gate is the real check

On disk _materials grows to length 6 with nulls at indices 1..4 and {__uuid__:plane} at [5] (setDeep does cur[5]=value, no bounds check). The interesting native question: does project_reimport_asset accept this? Likely it reimports (nulls are tolerated as empty material slots) OR logs a warning. RECORD the engine's behaviour as the finding — coir does NOT guard out-of-range array writes.
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset('db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab') → record success/failure (this is the validity gate; a hard failure would mean coir produced an invalid file). 2) If it reimports, prefab_instantiate_prefab + read soldier cc.SkinnedMeshRenderer — observe whether the renderer now has 6 material slots with empties. ASSERT either: (a) reimport fails → coir produced engine-invalid output (a real gap to report), or (b) reimport succeeds with a 6-slot materials array → document that out-of-range set-uuid silently pads the array. Either outcome is a finding about coir's missing bounds check.
tests.cleanup: node_delete_node(newRoot) if instantiated. Restore _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab.bak → _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab; project_reimport_asset(...). Verify restored.
tests.notes: Surfaces a real coir behaviour: setDeep has no array-bounds check, so [5] on a length-1 array extends it with nulls. This is the one case where the CLI says OK but the result may be semantically wrong — exactly what native verification exists to catch. Keep --backup for safe restore.

tests.title: concurrent-change guard: file touched after read → write REFUSED exit 2, file unchanged
tests.coirArgs: edit _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab set-active soldier_t/RootNode --bool false   (with the file's mtime bumped between load and commit)
tests.expectedObservation: exit 2, stderr matches /file changed on disk since it was read/; the prefab bytes are unchanged
tests.cleanup: restore the original bytes you re-wrote during the simulation (see notes) so the fixture is pristine.
tests.notes: writeAtomic re-stats just before rename and throws if mtimeMs !== expectMtime; commitOrExit catches → exit 2. SIMULATION: ops.runEdit captures doc.mtime at load; the CLI is one process, so to make the on-disk mtime differ from what was read you must mutate the file's mtime DURING the run. Cleanest harness approach: write a tiny driver that imports runEdit/commitWrites, calls runEdit (captures mtime), then `fs.utimesSync(absPath, future, future)` (or rewrite identical bytes to bump mtimeMs), then commitWrites(writes) and assert it throws the guard message + bytes unchanged. If only the CLI binary is available, this case is not reproducible single-shot (load+commit happen in one tick); document that and rely on the driver. Restore bytes after.

tests.expectedObservation: exit 0, stdout '✓ written'; the write lands despite the mtime mismatch (commitWrites passed expectMtime:null)
tests.cleanup: restore the prefab to pristine (from a kept pristine copy or .bak); reimport.
tests.notes: commitWrites({force:true}) → writeAtomic({expectMtime:null}) skips the re-stat. Same SIMULATION harness as mtime-guard-refuses (driver that calls runEdit then bumps mtime then commitWrites with force). Pair with mtime-guard-refuses to prove the guard and its override. Use a driver; keep a pristine copy for restore.

tests.title: OPTIONAL native cross-check: coir's tree hierarchy matches the engine-instantiated node tree
tests.expectedObservation: coir tree -o json lists soldier_t > RootNode > {soldier,Bip001,shield,sword,shield01} …; the engine builds the same hierarchy from disk.
tests.nativeSteps: 1) prefab_instantiate_prefab('db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab') → newRootUuid. 2) node_get_node_info(newRootUuid) → assert name 'soldier_t'; then scene_get_scene_hierarchy (or recursive node_get_node_info) to confirm RootNode is the sole child and RootNode's children are soldier/Bip001/shield/sword/shield01. 3) Assert engine child names/structure equal coir tree's parent→child relationships.
tests.cleanup: node_delete_node(newRootUuid); do NOT save the scene. No .bak to restore (read-only op wrote nothing).
tests.notes: Read-only ops need no editor to VERIFY coir output, but this corroborates that coir's structure view agrees with the engine's load. Optional / low priority; the cli-reread tree tests are authoritative for this group.

tests.title: Reparent a leaf node to a new parent; verify both _children lists + _parent engine-side
tests.expectedObservation: stdout: '_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab: set-parent soldier_t/RootNode/sword → under soldier_t/RootNode/shield' + 'written (backup saved)'. JSON form: {op:'set-parent', node:'soldier_t/RootNode/sword', newParent:'soldier_t/RootNode/shield', index:-1}. No __id__ renumbering (set-parent never adds/removes entries).
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset('db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab') — must succeed (validity gate; a malformed reparent would fail re-read). 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab('db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab') → newRootUuid. 3) scene_get_scene_hierarchy (or node_get_node_info on newRootUuid + walk) to locate the instantiated 'RootNode', 'shield', and 'sword' nodes under newRootUuid. ASSERT: (a) the node named 'sword' now has parent 'shield' (node_get_node_info(sword).parent === shield uuid, or shield's children includes sword); (b) 'shield'._children/childrenCount now contains exactly one child named 'sword'; (c) 'RootNode'._children no longer contains 'sword' (RootNode now has 4 children: soldier, Bip001, sword's old slot gone, sword removed → soldier/Bip001/shield/shield01... i.e. sword absent). Confirm sword's local position/rotation/scale are unchanged from before (set-parent does not touch _lpos/_lrot/_lscale).
tests.cleanup: node_delete_node(newRootUuid) to remove the instantiated node WITHOUT saving the scene; then restore _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab from soldier_t.prefab.bak (move/copy .bak back over the file, delete the .bak); project_reimport_asset('db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab') so the engine re-reads the restored original.
tests.notes: sword(#69) and shield(#64) are both leaf children of RootNode(#2), each with empty _children — clean, unambiguous paths. Engine-side the reparent is the strongest single test of set-parent: confirms detach from old parent's _children, attach to new parent's _children, and _parent rewrite all land correctly in the on-disk file the engine re-reads.

tests.title: Reparent with --index 0 inserts the node as the FIRST child of the new parent (sibling order)
tests.expectedObservation: stdout: 'set-parent soldier_t/RootNode/sword → under soldier_t/RootNode/Bip001 [0]'. JSON: {op:'set-parent', node:'soldier_t/RootNode/sword', newParent:'soldier_t/RootNode/Bip001', index:0}. setParent splices the new _children ref at position 0 (was: [Bip001 Footsteps, Bip001 Pelvis] → becomes [sword, Bip001 Footsteps, Bip001 Pelvis]).
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset('db://assets/_coirtest/soldier_t.prefab') — succeed. 2) prefab_instantiate_prefab(...) → newRootUuid. 3) Locate instantiated 'Bip001' node; read its ordered children (node_get_node_info(Bip001).children or scene_get_scene_hierarchy). ASSERT: Bip001 now has 3 children and the FIRST child (index 0) is named 'sword', followed by 'Bip001 Footsteps' then 'Bip001 Pelvis' (order preserved). ASSERT sword's parent === Bip001. ASSERT 'RootNode' no longer lists 'sword'.
tests.notes: Bip001(#8) has exactly 2 ordered children: Bip001 Footsteps(#9), Bip001 Pelvis(#11). --index 0 must place sword ahead of both. This is the only test that proves the siblingIndex splice (vs append). Note --index out of range / negative falls back to append per setParent(), but index 0 is in range so it splices.

tests.title: --index beyond _children length appends at the end (no crash, no out-of-range error)
tests.expectedObservation: Exit 0. setParent treats siblingIndex >= _children.length (and <0/null) as append → sword becomes the LAST child of Bip001. stdout shows '[99]' in the desc but the actual position is appended. JSON: {op:'set-parent', index:99}.
tests.nativeSteps: 1) project_reimport_asset(...) — succeed. 2) instantiate → newRootUuid. 3) Read Bip001's ordered children. ASSERT: Bip001 has 3 children in order [Bip001 Footsteps, Bip001 Pelvis, sword] — i.e. sword is LAST (index 99 clamped to append). ASSERT sword.parent === Bip001; RootNode no longer lists sword.
tests.notes: Documents the documented append-fallback branch (siblingIndex<0 || >=length → push). Distinguishes from --index 0. Optional but valuable for audit-grade coverage of the splice/append boundary.

tests.title: Refuse moving a node under its own descendant (cycle) — exit 2, file unchanged
tests.expectedObservation: stderr: '✗ cannot move a node into its own descendant'. Exit 2 (isDescendant guard). File bytes UNCHANGED (and with --dry-run nothing is even attempted to write). No editor needed.
tests.cleanup: None — refusal writes nothing. (Still confirm soldier_t.prefab bytes/mtime are identical before and after.)
tests.notes: RootNode(#2) is an ancestor of Bip001(#8), so this is a true cycle. Verified live: emits the descendant message at exit 2. Use --dry-run so even a (hypothetical) bug couldn't write; the guard fires before any write regardless. cli-exit verify (assert exit code + unchanged bytes) is sufficient; no MCP.

tests.title: Refuse moving a node into itself — exit 2, file unchanged
tests.expectedObservation: stderr: '✗ cannot move a node into itself'. Exit 2 (nodeIndex === newParentIndex guard, checked before isDescendant). File unchanged.
tests.notes: Distinct guard branch from the descendant-cycle case (self-move is the nodeIndex===newParentIndex short-circuit). Verified live emits 'into itself'.

tests.title: Moving the prefab ROOT under one of its children is refused (descendant-cycle fires first) — exit 2
tests.expectedObservation: stderr: '✗ cannot move a node into its own descendant' (NOT the 'cannot move the root node' message — every node in this prefab is a descendant of the root, so isDescendant short-circuits before the _parent-missing root check). Exit 2, file unchanged.
tests.notes: Audit-grade detail: the root-move guard ('cannot move the root node', triggered by node._parent being non-ref) is unreachable in this fixture because RootNode is a descendant of soldier_t, so the cycle check wins. Documents which guard actually fires. Verified live.

tests.title: Refuse reparenting ONTO a nested-prefab-instance node as the new parent — instance guard, exit 2
tests.expectedObservation: stderr: '✗ "#2" is (in) a nested prefab instance — edit its source prefab directly, not here'. Exit 2 (editableGuard on the PARENT index, pres.index = #2 the instance root). File unchanged. The instance-root node name is empty so the guard reports it as '#2'.
tests.notes: nested_t/[0] (#2) is a [prefab instance] node (PrefabInfo.instance != null). set-parent runs editableGuard on BOTH node and parent; here the parent is the instance → refused. Moving an editable node UNDER an instance is blocked. Verified live.

tests.title: Refuse reparenting an instance node ITSELF (as the node being moved) — instance guard, exit 2
tests.expectedObservation: stderr: '✗ "#2" is (in) a nested prefab instance — edit its source prefab directly, not here'. Exit 2 (editableGuard on res.index = the instance node #2). File unchanged.
tests.notes: Complement to set-parent-onto-instance-parent: here the MOVED node is the instance (#2), the new parent is the editable Node(#13). The node-side editableGuard fires. Both directions (move an instance / move onto an instance) are blocked. Verified live: same '#2' message.

tests.title: Unresolvable node selector → exit 2 with candidate node-path list
tests.expectedObservation: stderr: '✗ no node "soldier_t/NoSuch"' followed by an indented list of all valid node paths (candidates, capped at 20). Exit 2 (selError on the node selector resolution). File unchanged.
tests.notes: Selector resolution failure on the node arg. Verified live: prints 'no node' + candidate paths. Confirms resolution errors surface before any guard/mutation.

tests.title: Unresolvable NEW-PARENT selector → exit 2 with candidates
tests.expectedObservation: stderr: '✗ no node "soldier_t/NoParent"' + candidate paths. Exit 2 (selError on the parent selector — pres resolution). File unchanged.
tests.notes: Parallel to set-parent-node-not-found but exercises the parent-arg resolution path (the second resolveSelector). Ensures both selector resolutions are guarded.

tests.title: set-parent on a component selector (…:Type) → needNode, exit 2
tests.expectedObservation: stderr: '✗ "soldier_t:cc.SkeletalAnimation" must select a node for this op'. Exit 2 (res.kind !== 'node' → OM.needNode). File unchanged.
tests.notes: soldier_t(#1) carries a cc.SkeletalAnimation component (confirmed in tree). A component selector for the MOVED node must be rejected — set-parent only moves nodes. Verified live.

tests.title: set-parent with a component selector as the NEW PARENT → needNode, exit 2
tests.expectedObservation: stderr: '✗ "soldier_t:cc.SkeletalAnimation" must select a node for this op'. Exit 2 (pres.kind !== 'node' → OM.needNode on the parent). File unchanged.
tests.notes: Complements set-parent-target-is-component on the parent side. A component can't be a parent. Both args must be node selectors.

tests.title: set-parent on a non-prefab/scene asset (image) → notPrefab, exit 2
tests.expectedObservation: stderr: '✗ "model/helloWorld/folder-001/sky.png" is a image, not a prefab/scene — edit only touches prefab/scene files'. Exit 2 (resolveEditFile asset.type !== prefab/scene). File unchanged (it's a PNG and is never opened for writing).
tests.notes: File-target guard fires before any selector parsing. Verified live with the exact message. sky.png is the designated not-a-prefab/scene refusal target.

tests.title: Missing the new-parent positional → usage error, exit 1, file unchanged
tests.expectedObservation: stderr: 'set-parent needs: <file> set-parent <nodeSelector> <newParentSelector> [--index i]'. Exit 1 (cmdSetParent usage guard: !parentSel → process.exit(1)). File unchanged. NOTE this is exit 1 (usage), distinct from the exit-2 resolution/guard failures.
tests.notes: Arg-arity guard in the CLI layer (editCli cmdSetParent), not in ops.js. Verified live: exit 1 + usage line. Confirms the usage-vs-resolution exit-code split (1=usage/value, 2=resolution/guard).

tests.title: Non-numeric --index is parsed to null → treated as append (exit 0), not an error
tests.coirArgs: edit _coirtest/soldier_t.prefab set-parent soldier_t/RootNode/sword soldier_t/RootNode/shield --index abc --dry-run -o json
tests.expectedObservation: Exit 0. parseArgs: '--index abc' → parseInt NaN → flags.index = null → set-parent appends (index reported as -1 in json because params.index ?? -1). JSON: {op:'set-parent', node:'…/sword', newParent:'…/shield', index:-1, dryRun:true}. --dry-run → no write, file unchanged.
tests.notes: Documents that a non-numeric --index is silently coerced to null (append) rather than rejected — a deliberate parseArgs behavior (Number.isNaN→null). Pure cli-exit/json-shape check, no editor. If the audit considers silent coercion a defect, this test surfaces it.
